caption: This video captures a static presentation slide for a "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" course, specifically "Tutorial – 4". The slide focuses on the "Work/unit mass of gas compressed" and displays a mathematical formula: "W/m = T1(s1 - s2) - (h1 - h2)". Below the formula is a data table with columns labeled "1", "2", "f", and "g", providing values for pressure "p (bar)", temperature "T (K)", enthalpy "h (J/g)", and entropy "s (J/gK)". The table entries include pressures like "1.013" and "202.6", temperatures such as "300" and "77", and corresponding values for h and s. The slide features the "NPTEL" logo and the text "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" at the bottom. Across all frames, the content remains identical, with no visual changes or movements occurring, indicating a still shot of the lecture material.
